title: Assignments for Remote Services and Reports
breadcrumbs: Assignments for Remote Services and Reports
text: Assignments for Remote Services and Reports Assignments in the sections [SERVICES_EXEC] and [REPORTS_EXEC] direct the execution of each service or report component to a Uniface Server specified in the [PATHS] section. The path can specify a TCP/IP or TLS network path. Example: Services and Reports on TCP/IP Path In the following example, the client assignment file directs services and reports to a network path $NET1. This path opens a TCP connection to the host machine HOST1, where the Uniface Router uses the UST (server name) ASV to start a Uniface Server (an application server instance) using the assignment file asv.asn . The application server then locates the services and reports on the local file system. ; Client assignment file [PATHS] $NET1 = TCP:HOST1|?|?|ASV [SERVICES_EXEC] * = $NET1:* [REPORTS_EXEC] * = $NET1:* Copy The Uniface Router on HOST1 uses the server name ASV to start an application server instance using the assignment file asv.asn . ; urouter.asn - Uniface Router assignment file on HOST1 [SERVERS] ASV = userver.exe /asn=asv.asn DATA = userver.exe /asn=data.asn Copy Assignments in the [ENTITIES] and [RESOURCES] sections of the application server’s assignment file asv.asn direct entities to a data server on HOST1 (not shown in example) and services and reports to UAR files on the local file system. ; asv.asn - ASV assignment file on HOST1 [PATHS] $NET2 = TCP:HOST1|kaye|birdbrain|DATA [ENTITIES] *.* = $NET2:*.* [RESOURCES] d:/unifiles/services.uar d:/unifiles/reports.uar Copy Was this topic helpful?
